narrative.description: Super Monkey Ball (2001) is a acclaimed party title recognized in retro gaming lists for its distinctive mechanics, pacing, and lasting influence on the genre.
narrative.core_loop: Complete rapid micro-challenges and compete for high scores with friends.

mechanics.map_notes: Core microgame rapid challenges loop defines Super Monkey Ball's identity.
mechanics.expression: Short timed minigames rotate rapidly, each with unique one-shot rules.

mechanics.map_notes: Core score chain multiplier loop defines Super Monkey Ball's identity.
mechanics.expression: Consecutive successful inputs build a multiplier that boosts final score.

mechanics.map_notes: Core local multiplayer competition loop defines Super Monkey Ball's identity.
mechanics.expression: Same-screen or local wireless play pits players against each other in shared sessions.

mechanics.map_notes: Life Based Retry System supports the main loop in Super Monkey Ball.
mechanics.expression: A failure-buffer system in which the player is granted a limited number of retries, losing one life upon failure and restarting from a previous checkpoint.

mechanics.map_notes: Optional Content Layering supports the main loop in Super Monkey Ball.
mechanics.expression: Hidden rooms, passages, or content zones exist off main paths, requiring players to investigate suspicious environments.

mechanics.map_notes: Mission Select Structure supports the main loop in Super Monkey Ball.
mechanics.expression: A menu or hub lets the player choose missions or levels in flexible order with difficulty ratings.

mechanics.map_notes: Telegraphed Threat Loop supports the main loop in Super Monkey Ball.
mechanics.expression: A combat encounter structure in which an enemy signals an incoming attack through a distinct visual or audio cue before the attack resolves.

mechanics.map_notes: Input Aligned Movement supports the main loop in Super Monkey Ball.
mechanics.expression: Movement responds directly and immediately to input direction with minimal inertia, optimized for top-down or free-aim combat spaces.

mechanics.map_notes: Combo Trick Scoring supports the main loop in Super Monkey Ball.
mechanics.expression: Chained tricks and aerial maneuvers multiply score within a combo timer during skating or stunt gameplay.

mechanics.map_notes: Depletable Health Pool supports the main loop in Super Monkey Ball.
mechanics.expression: A survival mechanic where the player character possesses a finite quantity of health points that decrease when taking damage, with depletion resulting in failure.

mechanics.map_notes: Mid Level Checkpoint supports the main loop in Super Monkey Ball.
mechanics.expression: A save point within a stage resets the player to that position on death rather than restarting the entire level.

mechanics.map_notes: Fill Reward Progression Loop supports the main loop in Super Monkey Ball.
mechanics.expression: A progression gate where a fixed collection target must be met before an upgrade or skill point becomes available, tying advancement directly to measurable progress.

mechanics.map_notes: Patterned Boss Encounter supports the main loop in Super Monkey Ball.
mechanics.expression: A boss fight design in which the enemy executes a deterministic or semi-deterministic set of behaviors including telegraphed attacks and movement routines.

mechanics.map_notes: Multi Phase Boss Battle supports the main loop in Super Monkey Ball.
mechanics.expression: A combat structure where boss encounters transition through discrete stages with distinct attack patterns, behaviors, or environmental conditions when health thresholds are reached.

mechanics.map_notes: Linear Stage Gauntlet supports the main loop in Super Monkey Ball.
mechanics.expression: Progression moves through a fixed sequence of confined stages without hub backtracking.

design_intent.player_fantasy: A party experience centered on Super Monkey Ball.
